mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances, or unemployment spikes fail to predict negative returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal direction reverses and becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation and elevated policy rates create a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns exceed 55 percent when both thresholds are breached, or the joint condition fails to predict negative returns in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annually confirms robust aggregate demand, which immediately boosts equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above threshold fails to generate positive 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations, or signal loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment signals efficient labor reallocation and supply-side strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when unemployment declines alongside production, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant.
